commands.review_summary:
The PR expands markdownlint-cli2 coverage to shipped workspace templates and removes trailing punctuation from affected template headings.

PR surface: Docs 0, Other 0. Total 0 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a docs/tooling PR; source inspection confirms current main excludes the shipped docs templates from markdownlint and the PR expands that existing lint lane.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No repair lane is needed because there are no blocking review findings; the remaining action is ordinary maintainer and merge-gate handling.

target.title: fix(docs): lint workspace templates
target.body:
## Summary

* Extends the existing markdownlint docs lane to cover shipped workspace template markdown.
* Removes the explicit markdownlint exclusion for `docs/reference/templates/**`.
* Fixes the resulting template markdownlint failures without broad repo-wide markdown formatting churn.
* Intentionally keeps this PR scoped to issue #91160; unrelated docs cleanup, refactors, and changelog edits are out of scope.
* Reviewers should focus on whether the lint scope is correct and whether the markdown fixes are minimal.

## Linked context

Closes #91160

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No direct maintainer request. This follows the open issue and ClawSweeper’s suggested narrow implementation shape.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

* Behavior or issue addressed: The shipped workspace template markdown was excluded from the existing markdownlint docs lane.
* Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on my machine.
* Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
pnpm lint:docs
pnpm format:docs:check
pnpm check:docs
git diff --check
```

* Evidence after fix:

```text
$ pnpm lint:docs
$ pnpm dlx --config.resolution-mode=highest markdownlint-cli2 --config config/markdownlint-cli2.jsonc
markdownlint-cli2 v0.22.1 (markdownlint v0.40.0)
Finding: docs/**/*.md docs/**/*.mdx README.md !docs/zh-CN/** !docs/.i18n/** !**/.local/**
Linting: 675 file(s)
Summary: 0 error(s)

$ pnpm format:docs:check
$ node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check
Docs formatting clean (662 files).

$ pnpm check:docs
$ pnpm format:docs:check && pnpm lint:docs && pnpm docs:check-mdx && pnpm docs:check-i18n-glossary && pnpm docs:check-links
$ node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check
Docs formatting clean (662 files).
$ pnpm dlx --config.resolution-mode=highest markdownlint-cli2 --config config/markdownlint-cli2.jsonc
markdownlint-cli2 v0.22.1 (markdownlint v0.40.0)
Finding: docs/**/*.md docs/**/*.mdx README.md !docs/zh-CN/** !docs/.i18n/** !**/.local/**
Linting: 675 file(s)
Summary: 0 error(s)
$ node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs README.md
Docs MDX check passed (677 files, 12564ms).
$ node scripts/check-docs-i18n-glossary.mjs
$ node scripts/docs-link-audit.mjs
checked_internal_links=4445
broken_links=0

$ git diff --check
# no output
```

* Observed result after fix: The docs markdownlint lane now includes `docs/reference/templates/**` and completes without the template markdownlint errors.
* What was not tested: Full runtime behavior was not tested because this PR only changes markdownlint coverage and markdown formatting in docs/template files.
* Proof limitations or environment constraints: Validation is limited to local docs/tooling commands for the touched docs lint surface.
* Before evidence:

```text
markdownlint-cli2 v0.22.1 (markdownlint v0.40.0)
Finding: docs/**/*.md docs/**/*.mdx README.md !docs/zh-CN/** !docs/.i18n/** !**/.local/**
Linting: 675 file(s)
Summary: 6 error(s)
docs/CLAUDE.md:1:9 error MD047/single-trailing-newline Files should end with a single newline character
docs/reference/templates/AGENTS.md:51:41 error MD026/no-trailing-punctuation Trailing punctuation in heading [Punctuation: '!']
docs/reference/templates/AGENTS.md:87:26 error MD026/no-trailing-punctuation Trailing punctuation in heading [Punctuation: '!']
docs/reference/templates/AGENTS.md:113:26 error MD026/no-trailing-punctuation Trailing punctuation in heading [Punctuation: '!']
docs/reference/templates/AGENTS.md:142:32 error MD026/no-trailing-punctuation Trailing punctuation in heading [Punctuation: '!']
docs/reference/templates/CLAUDE.md:1:9 error MD047/single-trailing-newline Files should end with a single newline character
```

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

```bash
pnpm lint:docs
pnpm format:docs:check
pnpm check:docs
git diff --check
```

Regression coverage added or updated:

* Updated existing markdownlint coverage so shipped workspace templates are linted by the docs lane.

What failed before this fix, if known:

* Once the template ignore was removed, markdownlint reported MD026 heading punctuation failures in `docs/reference/templates/AGENTS.md`.
* Markdownlint also reported MD047 final-newline failures in `docs/reference/templates/CLAUDE.md` and `docs/CLAUDE.md`.

If no test was added, why not?

No new test was added because this change uses the existing markdownlint docs lane as the regression check.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `No`

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `Yes`

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`

What is the highest-risk area?

The markdownlint config now covers files that were previously excluded.

How is that risk mitigated?

The PR keeps the scope narrow, uses the existing markdownlint config and docs scripts, and fixes only the resulting markdownlint failures needed for the template files to pass.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Ready for review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Waiting on CI and maintainer review.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

No PR review comments yet.

AI-assisted: yes. I used ChatGPT to understand the issue scope and PR requirements. I reviewed the final changes myself and confirm I understand them.
